scope: Hard rule 'KuCoin spot agents cannot short' (KC-spot path only) — does NOT supersede the spot-rejection rule itself, which remains in force at the KC execution gate
corrected_assertion: Shorting is NOW AUTHORIZED via a new 3-stage progression on a separate Binance Futures path. Stage 1 (SHADOW): services/mae-futures-short/ Cloud Run service writes shadow_short_positions audit rows only, NO venue calls — runs for ≥14 days clean parity before progression. Stage 2 (testnet paper): switch shadow service to Binance Futures testnet (testnet.binancefuture.com), $0 real exposure, validate fill semantics + liquidation handling for ≥7 days. Stage 3 (real-money canary): $10 size positions on Binance Futures perps mainnet, max 3 concurrent, gated on (a) Plutus perps fine-tune COMPLETE, (b) operator capital ≥ $2K total, (c) 14-day clean SHADOW parity reporter. The original KC-spot hard rule remains in force on KuCoin spot agents (a5/a6/a8) — that rejection path is unchanged. Shorting capability lives ONLY on the new Binance Futures path with all three gates green. GH Project #31 items #219-#223 capture rollout work. Funding rate threshold: +0.05%/8h — above this, do NOT add to existing shorts. Liquidation price band: ≥15% above entry (asymmetric vs longs at 4% SL). Max leverage by regime: chop-day=1, trend-day=2, chop-active=1, news-day=0. Circuit breaker: on regime flip to news-day OR funding spike >+0.08%/8h → CLOSE_ALL_SHORTS market order, no override.
source: GH Project #31 items #219-#223 [GCP-CUTOVER · Shorts] + operator decision moment 2026-05-13 in session 24cbed9c
live_evidence: services/mae-futures-short/cmd/main.go (pending — blocked on Plutus perps fine-tune)
retention_policy: permanent — re-evaluate on capital threshold change OR Plutus perps fine-tune drift >5% OR Binance Futures geo-blocking US users
